excerpt: لاَ صَلوٰۃَ اِلاَّبِالطُّھُارۃ — Prayer is not without purity
source_location_hint: Foundational principle of Islamic ritual law

excerpt: Prayer is based upon four pillars; those who neglect them are deceivers — table of four fundamentals
When the verse [Qur'an 33:33] was revealed, the Messenger of God took Mawlānā ʿAlī, Mawlānā Fāṭima, and the two Imams beneath his cloak — the Five under the Cloak (Panj Tan-i ʿAẓīm)
source_author: Tradition of the Prophet; documented by al-Muʾayyad and Kirmani
source_location_hint: The verse of purification (Āyat al-Taṭhīr) Q33:33 narrative context

excerpt: He used to wash his head and feet in early Islam; after the Mi'rāj, he began to wipe them—his successor perceived the gesture and followed; opponents remained in the earlier practice
source_work: Qadi al-Nuʿman, Taʾwil al-Daʿaʾim; Kirmani esoteric exegesis
source_author: Compiled by Qadi al-Nuʿman; interpreted by Kirmani
source_location_hint: Distinction between washing (ghasl) and wiping (masḥ) in ablution practice

excerpt: Protective concealment is my religion and the religion of my fathers; yet three things cannot be concealed: intoxicants, wiping over socks, abandoning Bismillāh in prayer
source_work: Imam Jaʿfar al-Ṣādiq traditions; compiled in Qadi al-Nuʿman, Daʿaʾim al-Islam
source_authority: Qadi al-Nuʿman, Daʿaʾim al-Islam; Kirmani commentaries
source_location_hint: Three prohibitions under taqiyya (protective concealment); Islamic jurisprudential principle

excerpt: The earth is your mother and she brings you good — the Prophet pointed to his successor ʿAlī through these words
source_authority: Collected in Nahj al-Balagha (al-Sharīf al-Raḍī); Qadi al-Nuʿman, Daʿaʾim al-Islam
source_location_hint: Esoteric connection between earth metaphor and ʿAlī's succession
